summary: Iter67 Standardized Credit Policy backend delta verification — 15/15 pytest cases PASS (100%). Confirmed: `ACTION_COST=99` in server.py; `get_action_cost()` returns 99 for BOTH fresher and experienced students (previously 199 for experienced). /api/subscription/plans.paid_tier.action_cost=99 and /api/wallet.action_cost=99 for both categories. Fresher & Experienced job-application on a pro-posted job each deduct 99 credits; applications.credits_charged persisted as 99. Fresher & Experienced mock interview booking each deduct 99 credits; interview_slots.credits_charged=99. Insufficient credits gate (98 credits) blocks both apply (402) and book (400) with 'Insufficient credits' message; no rows written, no deduction. Free-use pass still consumed first (no deduction, free_uses_left decrements). Admin walk-in job apply rejected with 400 for both categories — zero credit deduction, no application row. Pro job_post_reward=+200 fires exactly once at the 4th valid application (no double-payment on 5th). Note: /api/jobs/apply returns HTTP 402 for insufficient credits (spec allows 'or equivalent'); /api/interviews/book returns 400 — both surface the same 'Insufficient credits' detail message.

test_matrix.title: GET /subscription/plans (experienced) → paid_tier.action_cost == 99 (NOT 199)
test_matrix.notes: Delta from prior iters where experienced was 199

test_matrix.title: Fresher apply to pro job → wallet -99; applications.credits_charged=99
Experienced apply to pro job → wallet -99 (NOT -199); applications.credits_charged=99
Fresher with 98 credits → apply blocked (402) 'Insufficient credits'; no app row
Experienced with 98 credits → apply blocked (402) 'Insufficient credits'; no app row
Fresher with 98 credits → book blocked (400) 'Insufficient credits'; slot rolled back
Experienced with 98 credits → book blocked (400) 'Insufficient credits'
Fresher with free_uses_left=1 → free consumed first, credits unchanged, slot.credits_charged=0
Fresher applies to admin walk-in → 400 'Admin Walk-in…'; no deduction, no app row
Experienced applies to admin walk-in → 400; no deduction, no app row
Pro +200 job_post_reward fires exactly once at 4th application (not on 5th); reason='job_post_reward' tx present

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 402 for insufficient credits while POST /api/interviews/book returns HTTP 400 for the same condition. Both share the identical detail 'Insufficient credits. Please add credits…'. Not a spec violation (spec says 400 or equivalent), but the inconsistency is worth harmonising for a cleaner API contract.

action_items: Optional: consider aligning HTTP status for 'Insufficient credits' between /jobs/apply (402) and /interviews/book (400) for API consistency.

Stale test file `/app/backend/tests/test_iter51_credit_overhaul.py` still hard-codes EXPERIENCED_COST=199 and JOB_POST_REWARD assertions against pre-Iter67 behaviour — will FAIL as regression when re-run. Recommend either (a) update its EXPERIENCED_COST constant to 99, or (b) delete it in favour of the new Iter67 file.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:155-156 keeps ACTION_COST_FRESHER / ACTION_COST_EXPERIENCED as backward-compat aliases both pointing to 99 — good defensive shim so existing imports (e.g., analytics) don't break. Recommend a follow-up cleanup pass to delete the aliases and inline ACTION_COST everywhere to eliminate confusion.
server.py:170-171 get_action_cost() returns 0 for non-students. Confirmed still correct — pros/employers/admin do not consume credits on booking/applying (they never can, but the code path is safe).
server.py:257 REVENUE_PER_ACTION_INR now == 99. Downstream analytics computing revenue per action should update their assumptions if they cached the old 199. No blocker.
routers/jobs.py:513 uses `<` comparator (correct — 98<99 blocks, 99>=99 allows). routers/interviews.py:278 identical. Both consistent.
routers/jobs.py:514 raises 402 (Payment Required) while interviews.py:284 raises 400. Consider unifying to 402 across the wallet-gated actions.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral: signed up fresh students (T67_FR, T67_EX, T67_LOW_*, T67_FREE, T67_APP0..4) and one professional (T67_PRO) per test class through /api/auth/signup + verify-otp; profile completed via direct Mongo update; pro-posted jobs and interview_slots inserted directly in mongo with last_date_to_apply=today+30. Module-scoped autouse teardown cleans up t67_* users, T67 jobs, applications, and TEST interview_slots.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter67 standardization is green end-to-end on backend. Reusable regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter67_standardized_credits.py (15 tests, ~38s). Note: pre-existing test file /app/backend/tests/test_iter51_credit_overhaul.py still asserts EXPERIENCED_COST=199 — it WILL fail post-Iter67 and should be updated or retired. /api/jobs/apply returns 402 for insufficient credits (spec-tolerant equivalent to 400). Profile-complete gate (Iter58) is real — any test needing to hit /jobs/apply or /interviews/book must fully populate profile fields, not just profile_complete=True; the helper `_set_profile_role` in this test file demonstrates the required set (name, phone+phone_verified, gender, dob, education, passed_out_year, skills[], current_location, resume_link).
rca_of_issue: N/A — no failing scenarios; the standardization holds across all 9 spec matrix rows and additional wallet-parity/free-use coverage.
